task_completion.description: Log into the Django Administration panel
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Django administration dashboard is visible, indicating successful login as admin.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Group page
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add group' page in the Django admin interface is open, showing the Name field and Permissions selection boxes.

task_completion.description: Enter the new user group name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text input field for 'Name' contains exactly 'Personal Trainers'.

task_completion.description: Assign the three required exercise permissions
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Chosen permissions' box (usually on the right side of the permissions widget) contains 'exercises | exercise | Can add exercise', 'exercises | exercise | Can change exercise', and 'exercises | exercise | Can view exercise'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the delete permission is not assigned
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Chosen permissions' box does NOT contain 'exercises | exercise | Can delete exercise', and ideally contains no other permissions besides the three required ones.
A success message or banner (usually green) indicating that the group 'Personal Trainers' was added successfully, or the group is visible in the list of existing groups.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with the application
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window is visible displaying the wger Django admin interface, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the permission selection widget
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent selecting permissions from the 'Available permissions' list and moving them to the 'Chosen permissions' list using the UI arrows.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database or shell manipulation
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are open showing SQLite/PostgreSQL prompts, Django shell (manage.py shell), or Python scripts modifying the database directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected group name is exactly 'Personal Trainers'. The group must have exactly 3 permissions assigned in the UI: 'exercises | exercise | Can add exercise', 'exercises | exercise | Can change exercise', and 'exercises | exercise | Can view exercise'. It must explicitly NOT have 'exercises | exercise | Can delete exercise'.
